text: **Anna:** Hi there!
**Anna:** {Summer|夏/季節} in Washington DC is {hot|暑い/高温} and {sunny|晴れた/日差しのある}.
**Anna:** I always {ride|乗る/利用する} the {metro|地下鉄} to work.
**Anna:** Riding the metro is {cool|涼しい/格好いい} and fast.
**Anna:** But today it's closed.
**Anna:** So I am {walking|歩く} to work.
**Anna:** Ms.
**Anna:** Weaver, I am late this morning.
**Anna:** The metro is closed.
**Anna:** So I am walking to work.
**Caty:** That's too bad.
**Caty:** It's really {hot|暑い/熱い}.
**Anna:** Yes, it is.
**Anna:** When you {arrive|到着する/着く}, please come to my office.
**Caty:** I have {important|重要な/大切な} news to tell you.
**Anna:** Of course.
**Anna:** Goodbye.
**Anna:** My boss has news for me.
**Anna:** The {question|質問/疑問} is, is it good news or bad news?
**Anna:** Hello, Ms.
**Anna:** Weaver.
**Caty:** Anna, I have good news and I have bad news.
**Caty:** Which do you want to hear first?
**Anna:** The good news.
**Anna:** No Okay, the bad news.
**Caty:** The bad news is you are not good at {reading|読む} the news.
**Anna:** Oh, I am very sorry to hear that.
**Anna:** So, starting next month, you will not read the news.
**Anna:** Next month is July
**Caty:** You are {firing|解雇する/首にする} me in July.
**Caty:** No, I am not {firing|解雇する/首にする|/ˈfaɪərɪŋ/|He was firing workers last month.} you in July.
**Caty:** Or in August or in September.
**Anna:** That is the good news.
**Anna:** Okay.
**Anna:** You are not {firing|解雇する/首にする} me
**Anna:** I am not reading the news.
**Anna:** What will I be doing?
**Caty:** Well, you are good at {asking|尋ねる/質問する|/ˈæskɪŋ/|She is asking a question.} questions.
**Caty:** You are good at {talking|話す/語る|/ˈtɔːkɪŋ/|They are talking about work.} to people.
**Caty:** You are good at {showing|示す/見せる|/ˈʃoʊɪŋ/|He is showing his passport.} your feelings.
**Caty:** And you are great at being {silly|馬鹿げた/おかしな|/ˈsɪli/|Don't be silly!}.
**Anna:** Thank you, Ms.
**Anna:** Weaver.
**Anna:** But what does all that mean?
**Caty:** I have a new {assignment|課題/仕事|/əˈsaɪnmənt/|She received a new assignment.} for you.
**Anna:** Your skills are {perfect|完璧な/完全な|/ˈpɜːrfɪkt/|That is a perfect solution.} for a new show.
**Anna:** A {children's|子供の} show.
**Anna:** That is awesome.
**Anna:** When do I start?
**Caty:** You start next month.
**Anna:** Start {thinking|考える/思考する|/ˈθɪŋkɪŋ/|Start thinking about your future.} of ideas for the show.
**Anna:** I have {tons|たくさん/大量|/tʌnz/|I have tons of homework.} of ideas.
**Anna:** I can show children what it's like in {outer|外側の/外部の|/ˈaʊtər/|Outer space is vast.} space
**Anna:** Or great in the deep dark {ocean|海/大洋|/ˈoʊʃən/|The ocean is very deep.}.
**Caty:** Those are great ideas, Anna.
**Caty:** Please go think of more.
**Caty:** At your desk.
**Anna:** Yes.
**Anna:** What other things can I show them?
**Anna:** {Mount|山/山岳|/maʊnt/|Mount Everest is very high.} Everest.
**Anna:** Everyone has {different|異なる/違う|/ˈdɪfərənt/|We have different colors.} skills.
**Caty:** You have skills, I have skills.
**Caty:** The {important|重要な|/ɪmˈpɔːrtənt/|This is an important decision.} thing is to know what you are good at.
**Anna:** Until next time.
